data.title: As a DSO maintainer, I add Apache-2.0 NOTICE and README attribution
data.description:
## Description

What: Add a NOTICE file satisfying Apache-2.0 section 4 attribution and link it from README so the forks derivation is legally documented.
Why: Apache-2.0 compliance is a legal requirement when distributing a derivative work.
Scope:
- IN: Create NOTICE at repo root; preserve upstream LICENSE unmodified; link NOTICE from the DSO-baked README.
- OUT: License text changes; non-Apache-2.0 code; upstream content outside LICENSE/README.

## Done Definitions

- When complete, NOTICE at repo root documents (a) upstream fork source (navapbc/template-application-nextjs) at a pinned upstream commit SHA, (b) derivation date, (c) modifications made (copier render, nava-platform strip, DSO infra addition), (d) section 4 attribution of the original Apache-2.0 work.
  Satisfies: Apache-2.0 section 4 attribution (SC6)
- When complete, the DSO-baked README contains a visible link to NOTICE.
  Satisfies: SC6
- When complete, upstream LICENSE is preserved unmodified.
  Satisfies: SC6

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Apache-2.0 section 4 is a legal requirement; have a stakeholder review NOTICE content before merging.
- [Maintainability] If the fork is re-rendered from a newer upstream in future, NOTICE must be updated to reflect the new upstream SHA and modifications list. Document this drift hazard in NOTICE itself.

## Escalation Policy

Escalation policy: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue - one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
